items.title: Detailed Change List for Task-Aware Router v2 in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a comprehensive change list for the v2 task-aware orchestrator design, detailing specific files and functions that require modification. Key additions include new modules for the failover-router in Go, updates to three channel workers, and adjustments to the PlatformGateway. The documentation is purely informational, with no impact on existing code or production traffic.

items.title: Revamped Async Orchestrator Design in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a comprehensive design for the task-aware async orchestrator in the failover-router. Key changes include the router managing public task IDs and callbacks, ensuring synchronous attempts for downstream workers, and implementing robust error handling. The design aims to enhance reliability and traceability in async operations, addressing limitations of the previous version.

items.title: Update vue-i18n to version 11.4.6 in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update bumps the vue-i18n library from version 11.4.5 to 11.4.6. Key changes include improvements and bug fixes that enhance internationalization support in the application. The package-lock and package.json files have been updated accordingly to reflect this change.

items.title: Update autoprefixer to latest version in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update bumps the autoprefixer package from version 10.5.0 to 10.5.1. The new version includes a fix for the grid-area span reset when overriding areas, enhancing layout consistency. This change ensures better compatibility and performance in the frontend code.

items.title: Update axios to version 1.18.1 in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update bumps axios from version 1.18.0 to 1.18.1, addressing several bug fixes and enhancing runtime and type correctness. Key changes include improvements to the Node HTTP adapter and better error serialization, which will enhance the stability and performance of the application. Additionally, documentation has been updated for clarity and new contributors have been welcomed.
